LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE HARDSHIP PLAINTIFF WILL SUFFER, THE LOSS OF HIS HOME, IS FAR GREATER THAN THE HARM DEFENDANTS ARE LIKELY TO SUFFER IF THE INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT HE WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF SHALL POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF IS TO CONTINUE TO PAY THE MORTGAGE, TAXES, INSURANCE, AND A MONTHLY PAYMENT (AMOUNT STATED ON THE RECORD) TO DEFENDANT. THIS RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ANYONE'S RIGHTS FOR ARGUMENTS IN THIS MATTER SUBJECT TO BEING DELEGATED LATER BY WHATEVER THE PROCEEDING IS, WHETHER IT IS ARBITRATION OR A CIVIL CASE. PARTIES DO NOT WAIVE ANY OF THEIR RIGHTS. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. Appearances: Steven L. Hammond of Hammond Law (955.1915) for plaintiff and moving party; Mark J. Romeo of the Law Offices of Mark J. Romeo (395.9315) for defendants and responding parties; Leena Rege of Holme Roberts & Owen, LLP (268.1919) for defendant Chicago Title. Argued and the Court

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: Preliminary injunction is granted. The hardship plaintiff will suffer, the loss of his home, is far greater than the harm defendants are likely to suffer if the injunction is denied. Plaintiff demonstrates a reasonable probability that he will prevail on the merits. Plaintiff shall post a bond in the amount of $1,000. In addition, plaintiff is to continue to pay the mortgage, taxes, insurance, and a monthly payment (amount stated on the record) to defendant. This ruling is without prejudice to anyone's rights for arguments in this matter subject to being delegated later by whatever the proceeding is, whether it is arbitration or a civil case. Parties do not waive any of their rights. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
